BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin on whether my CS tutoring and used-textbook resale cover the software, printing, and gear I spend on each month. Trying to decide if the side income is worth the time during the semester.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. I'll revisit once fall-semester numbers settle. Textbook resale swings a lot month to month.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: If I start paying for a small hosting plan and print more cheat sheets, my monthly costs go up by about $25.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $35 + $25 = $60. At $25 per weekly student, $60 / $25 = 2.4 -> 3 regular students needed to break even.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Easily covered at 5 regular students plus drop-ins.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - I add a paid software subscription and costs jump by about $50/mo.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Even at 4 students I break even, and I usually have 5 regulars.

ActionItems: Track textbook resale income for one more month before committing to any paid software.
Revisit after fall finals once tutoring demand settles.
BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is only about 3-4 tutoring students at current costs. With 5 regulars plus drop-ins I have a comfortable buffer. Main risk is textbook resale drying up, but that's a small slice of the total.
